title: The Survival of Ancient Languages
description: For questions 17 – 24, read the text below. Use the word given in capitals at the end of some of the lines to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: The world is home to thousands of languages, each representing a unique (0) linguistic system developed over thousands of years. The study of ancient languages provides (17) historical insight into how early civilisations thought and organised their societies. The (18) fascination with deciphering ancient scripts such as Egyptian hieroglyphics remains strong among archaeologists. Language is a deeply (19) cultural inheritance, carrying the stories, beliefs, and values of generations past. Tragically, many ancient languages now face (20) extinction as communities abandon them in favour of global languages. Today, there are only a handful of native (21) speakers of some of the world's rarest languages. Active efforts for the (22) preservation of endangered languages involve recording vocabulary and creating educational materials. The loss of any language represents a (23) significant blow to human knowledge and diversity. Without urgent action, the (24) communication channels linking us to ancient civilisations may be permanently closed.

example.text: The world is home to thousands of languages, each representing a unique (0) LINGUISTIC system. (LINGUIST)

questions.explanation: An adjective is needed before 'insight'; 'history' becomes 'historical' by adding the suffix '-ical'.
A noun is needed after 'The'; 'fascinate' becomes 'fascination' by replacing '-ate' with '-ation'.
An adjective is needed before 'inheritance'; 'culture' becomes 'cultural' by adding the suffix '-al'.
A noun is needed after 'face'; 'extinct' becomes 'extinction' by adding the suffix '-ion'.
A plural noun is needed after 'native'; 'speak' becomes 'speaker' with the suffix '-er', then pluralised.
A noun is needed after 'the'; 'preserve' becomes 'preservation' by replacing '-ve' with '-vation'.
An adjective is needed before 'blow'; 'signify' becomes 'significant' by changing '-ify' to '-icant'.
A noun is needed before 'channels'; 'communicate' becomes 'communication' by replacing '-ate' with '-ation'.
